title: Health Endpoint Basic Check
description: Verify that health check endpoints GET /health and GET /api/health respond with HTTP 200 status and report system operational status including database connectivity, event store initialization, and core engine availability.

steps.description: Send GET request to /health endpoint on port 4320
Verify response status code is 200
Verify response body reports database connectivity as healthy
Verify response body indicates event store is initialized
Verify response body indicates core engine is available
Send GET request to /api/health endpoint on port 4320
Verify response status code is 200
Verify response body reports all system components healthy

title: Event Store Append Event Success
description: Validate that appending a new event to the event store succeeds, and that the event is persisted with correct data and meta information.

steps.description: Construct a valid event payload with unique event ID and required fields
Append event to the event store via CoreExecutor interface
Verify event store accepts and persists the event
Verify event is assigned correct stream version and hash chain is updated

title: Event Store Reject Duplicate Events
description: Ensure that event store rejects duplicate events based on event ID to enforce idempotency and prevent duplicate entries.

steps.description: Append an event with a unique event ID to the event store
Verify event is persisted successfully
Attempt to append another event with the same event ID
Verify event store rejects the second event as a duplicate
Verify error or rejection mechanism indicates idempotency enforcement

title: Event Replay Produces Consistent State
description: Verify that replaying all events from the event store deterministically reproduces current system state without drift.

steps.description: Retrieve all events from the event store in correct order
Replay events to rebuild operational state projection
Verify that rebuilt projection state matches current live state exactly
Verify no state differences or drift exist after replay

title: Event Store Stream Versioning Integrity
description: Validate that event stream versioning increments properly with each event and prevents invalid version overwrites.

steps.description: Append multiple events sequentially to a single event stream
Verify each event stream version increases by 1 sequentially
Attempt to append an event with a stream version lower or equal to last known version
Verify event store rejects the event due to version conflict

title: Session Start from INACTIVE to ACTIVE
description: Test starting a new session changes its state from INACTIVE to ACTIVE and records the session start event in event store atomically.

steps.description: Invoke session start operation via CoreExecutor for an INACTIVE session
Verify session state transitions to ACTIVE
Verify session start event is appended to event store
Verify session state change and event appending is atomic (all or none)

title: Session Close from ACTIVE to CLOSED
description: Verify closing an ACTIVE session transitions it to CLOSED state and records event accordingly. Closed sessions reject new order requests.

steps.description: Invoke session close on an ACTIVE session
Verify session state changes to CLOSED
Verify session closed event is recorded in event store
Attempt to create or add new order to the CLOSED session
Verify system rejects new orders for CLOSED session

title: Order Creation Initial State OPEN
description: Test that creating a new order sets its initial state to OPEN and allows subsequent item additions.

steps.description: POST /api/orders to create a new order
Verify response indicates order state is OPEN
Add items to the created order via PATCH /api/orders/{orderId}
Verify items are accepted and associated with the order

title: Order Lock State Transition and Immutability
description: Verify that locking an order transitions its state to LOCKED with total calculated and that order details cannot be modified thereafter.

steps.description: Create and add items to an OPEN order
Invoke locking of the order (transition OPEN → LOCKED)
Verify order state is LOCKED
Verify total amount is correctly calculated and stored
Attempt to add or modify items after order is LOCKED
Verify system rejects modifications to the LOCKED order

title: Order Close State Transition and Event Generation
description: Ensure that an order can be closed (LOCKED → CLOSED), events are generated for state transitions, and no further changes are allowed.

steps.description: Lock an existing order
Send POST /api/orders/{orderId}/close to close the order
Verify order state transitions to CLOSED
Verify order state change event is appended to event store
Attempt modifications on the CLOSED order
Verify system rejects any modifications

title: Order Cancellation Flow
description: Test that an order can be canceled from appropriate states and that cancellation event is recorded properly, preventing further changes.

steps.description: Create and lock an order
Perform order cancellation operation
Verify order state is CANCELED
Verify order canceled event is recorded in event store
Attempt any further order updates
Verify updates are rejected on canceled order

title: Total Calculation Accuracy and Immutability After Lock
description: Validate the correctness of order total calculation upon locking and ensure total does not change after locking.

steps.description: Create order and add various items with known prices and quantities
Lock the order to trigger total calculation
Verify calculated total matches expected sum of all items
Attempt to modify items and prices post-lock
Verify total remains unchanged and modifications are rejected

title: Session State Transitions Are Atomic
description: Test that session state transitions (INACTIVE → ACTIVE → CLOSED) and related event recordings occur atomically without partial completion.

steps.description: Start a new session transitioning INACTIVE to ACTIVE
Verify session state and event store are consistent
Close the session transitioning ACTIVE to CLOSED
Verify session closed state and event recorded atomically

title: Verify State Consistency After Concurrent Operations
description: Simulate concurrent session or order operations to verify that state remains consistent, no race conditions, and events are correctly ordered and persisted.

steps.description: Initiate multiple concurrent state-changing operations on the same order or session
Verify event store records events in correct order without conflict
Verify no inconsistent state or partial transition in system state
